raw_comment:
CATCHY TITLE// Racial Diversity: Sideline Statistics 

looking at these graphs I notice that there are higher percentages of players of color than there are coaches of color for every sport listed. I know that coaches stay in the league longer than players do and that there are more players than coaches. it just makes me wonder why POC don't get the representation that they deserve in higher positions, not just in sports but in any white-dominated industry. The impact that this could have on people in my community is devastating. I myself have watched sports my whole life and I used to dream of being a basketball coach in the WNBA. I never thought that I couldn't reach my goal because I always saw people who looked just like me doing it. Now I have come to realize that my fellow teammates, classmates, and friends had doubts as children because they never saw people who looked like them doing what they dreamed of, so they thought that they weren't allowed or that they didn't want people who looked like them to be in those positions. I can only imagine what it would feel like to have a dream but believe you couldn't reach it because of the skin color that you were born with. I think that these sats are a prime example of the argument that there should be more representation and equality in sports and positions of high regard.
ace_sentences: The title is Racial Diversity: Sideline Statistics.
I look at these graphs.
I notice that there are higher percentages of players of color than there are coaches of color for every sport listed.
I know that coaches stay in the league longer than players do.
I know that there are more players than coaches.
I wonder why POC do not get the representation that they deserve in higher positions.
I wonder why POC do not get the representation that they deserve in any white-dominated industry.
This could have a devastating impact on people in my community.
I have watched sports my whole life.
I used to dream of being a basketball coach in the WNBA.
I never thought that I could not reach my goal.
I always saw people who looked just like me doing it.
Now I have come to realize that my fellow teammates had doubts as children.
Now I have come to realize that my classmates had doubts as children.
Now I have come to realize that my friends had doubts as children.
They never saw people who looked like them doing what they dreamed of.
They thought that they were not allowed.
They thought that they did not want people who looked like them to be in those positions.
A person can have a dream.
A person can believe that the person could not reach the dream because of the skin color that the person was born with.
I think that these sats are a prime example of the argument.
I think that there should be more representation in sports.
I think that there should be more equality in sports.
I think that there should be more representation in positions of high regard.
I think that there should be more equality in positions of high regard.
